_note: REACTION INPUT-CONTROL TEST v2 (Brave=10, chicken-safe). v1 used Brave=0 and turned every unit into a CHICKEN on its turn (FFT panic state) - which itself PROVED the engine honors our live Brave write. RE found the chicken threshold at 0x30A9BD: 'cmp [unit+0x2B],10; jae normal' => Brave<10 chickens, Brave>=10 is safe. So we now force Brave=10 (the safe floor): no chicken, and the Brave%-gated reaction roll (trigger%=Brave at the 0x30BExx cluster) drops to ~10%, i.e. the reaction almost never fires. Same live-write mechanism as the proven evade test: the poller writes Brave on every unit's LIVE struct every ~20ms (TargetCharId=-1 all units; sweep=32 covers untracked defenders), BEFORE the reaction roll reads it. SETUP: a DEFENDER that owns a Brave%-gated reaction (Counter, BLADE GRASP, Hamedo, Arrow Guard...). RUN: relaunch the game. ATTACK that defender with a physical melee SEVERAL times (5-6). OBSERVE: at Brave=10 the reaction should fire almost NEVER (vs normally often). PASS (suppress confirmed) = reaction nearly stops + damage applies; log shows [BRAVE-OVERRIDE ... set(2B=0A)] and NO chicken. For a crisp second confirmation, tell me and I will flip to Brave=100 (force) where the reaction should fire on EVERY hit. Safe: struct writes only, no result forging. Evade override OFF to isolate reactions.
